mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the equity discount rate and relaxes financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is falling than when it is flat or rising, or if the sign-based hit rate falls below 55% in easing windows over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and future earnings risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, consumers are spending more and firms are hiring, indicating broad demand strength that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-positive RSAFS and PAYEMS growth does not outperform single-series conditions, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO without matching strength in PAYEMS signals weakening industrial activity and margin pressure, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO accompanied by weak PAYEMS does not produce negative 20-day forward returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% signals restrictive policy, and when CPIAUCSL is still elevated the market should expect tighter real financing conditions and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes with elevated CPIAUCSL do not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction fails to improve hit rate over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in HOUST marks improving housing demand and a stronger interest-sensitive growth impulse, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST is not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX performance, or if the forward-return sign is not positive in a majority of housing-upturn observations.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment regime with UNRATE near 4.7% or lower supports household income and consumption stability, so the market should trend upward over the next 20 trading days unless inflation is simultaneously re-accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE observations do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 combined with weak RSAFS indicates demand deceleration that will be reflected in lower corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in slowdown regimes over any 3-year rolling window.
